summary_findings: False-success prevention exists in commander/docs, but visible enforcement is still launch-count heavy and does not clearly require completion/atom/artifact integrity for success.
Partial rules are documented for under-launch, yet banned-model/unavailable-model paths and bundle-completeness enforcement are not clearly specified in reviewed docs.
Bundle/report shapes do not capture partial/failure reasons rigorously enough to audit missing artifacts or validation failures.
Monitor completion math can overstate completeness because any result JSON counts as done and filesystem-derived totals can shrink.
blockers: swarm_progress.py uses a fixed .tmp-swarm-state.json path, so parallel launch_requested calls raced; sequential retries were needed.
Leaf task launches did not expose background agent IDs through the task tool, so launch_started telemetry was recorded without agent_id.
consensus_notes: All five workers agreed the repo documents no-silent-downgrade expectations.
Multiple workers independently identified gaps between documented success/partial rules and concrete artifact/count enforcement.
conflict_notes: No material conflicts across worker findings; differences were scope-specific between documentation, commander contract, launcher, and monitor behavior.
